phases.name: CRM Foundation & Outlook Integration

workstreams.name: CRM Architecture & Setup
tasks: CRM-01: Internal prep (review scope, prep agenda, build data template)
CRM-02: Kickoff call with Trialta (Austin, Sam, Blake)
CRM-03: Document decisions & send data import template
CRM-04: Configure HubSpot environment & Trialta branding
CRM-05: Build contact properties (role, PE affiliation, strength) -- up to 20 custom properties
CRM-06: Build company properties & PE firm record types
CRM-07: Configure contact/company associations & labels (PE firm to portfolio company mapping)
CRM-08: Create segmentation views (PE vs sell-side vs portco)

workstreams.name: Outlook Integration
tasks: INT-01: Set up Outlook integration for 3 users (Austin, Sam, Blake -- Office 365)
INT-02: Test email sync, meeting sync, auto-contact creation
INT-03: Outlook training & enablement (30-min session)

workstreams.name: Data Prep & Import Mapping
tasks: MIG-01: Receive & audit raw data files from team (DEPENDENCY: Team submits data by end of W2)
MIG-02: Build import mapping -- map spreadsheet columns to HubSpot properties (contacts, companies, deals)

successCriteria: HubSpot portal configured with Trialta branding and up to 20 custom contact/company properties
PE firm to portfolio company parent-child associations are working with labeled relationship types
All 3 users have Outlook integration syncing email, calendar, and auto-creating contacts
Data import mapping documented and import template sent to client team
Segmentation views (PE vs sell-side vs portco) accessible to all users

phases.name: Data Migration & Pipeline Configuration

workstreams.name: Data Migration & Cleanup
tasks: MIG-03: Clean & deduplicate contact records (500-1K contacts across 3 lists -- RISK: biggest variable)
MIG-04: Clean & map company records
MIG-05: Import contacts & companies into HubSpot
MIG-06: Build PE firm to portfolio company associations post-import
MIG-07: Import historical deal records with PE attribution
MIG-08: Assign contact/deal ownership (Austin, Sam, Blake)

workstreams.name: Pipeline & Deal Management
tasks: CRM-09: Post-import QA & validation (spot-check associations, dedup review)
CRM-10: Team review & sign-off on imported data (async or 30-min call)
CRM-11: Configure buy-side pipeline & stages (Lead, Qualified, Proposal, Engaged, Completed)
CRM-12: Configure sell-side pipeline & stages
CRM-13: Build deal properties -- up to 25 custom (engagement type, characteristics)
CRM-14: Configure deal associations (PE firm, portco, contacts)
CRM-15: Set up association labels & test with sample deals
CRM-16: Pipeline walkthrough with team (30 min)

successCriteria: All contacts and companies imported with less than 5% duplicate rate
PE firm to portfolio company associations reflect actual portfolio structure
Historical deal records imported with correct PE attribution and ownership
Buy-side and sell-side pipelines configured with defined stages and required fields
Deal associations working -- PE firm, portco, and contacts linked per deal
Team has walked through both pipelines and confirmed stage definitions

phases.name: Health Tracking, Reporting & Go-Live

workstreams.name: Relationship Health Tracking
tasks: CRM-17: Build PE firm health properties (last engagement date, last contact date)
CRM-18: Configure 'Days Since Last Engagement' calculated property
CRM-19: Build PE firm views (active / warm / cold / dormant)
CRM-20: Set up 90-day automated task creation (auto-assign to PE firm relationship owner)
CRM-21: Build saved filters for at-risk partnerships

workstreams.name: Reporting & Dashboards
tasks: CRM-22: Build primary sales dashboard (~8 reports: pipeline velocity, PE sponsor value, engagement metrics)
CRM-23: Build PE firm engagement health dashboard
CRM-24: Configure deal conversion & pipeline reports

workstreams.name: QA & Handoff
tasks: UAT-01: End-to-end UAT walkthrough (test all workflows with real scenarios)
UAT-02: Fix/adjust based on UAT feedback (buffer for revisions & edge cases)
UAT-03: Formal handoff & begin 1-week async support (Slack/email through W7)

workstreams.name: Training & Enablement
tasks: TRN-01: Team enablement session -- full system walkthrough (60-90 min, recorded)
TRN-02: Create quick-reference documentation (daily CRM usage guide, key workflows)

successCriteria: PE firm health views showing active/warm/cold/dormant status based on engagement recency
90-day automated task creation triggering correctly for relationship owners
Sales dashboard live with pipeline velocity, PE sponsor value, and engagement metrics
UAT complete with all critical issues resolved
Team trained on daily CRM usage, pipeline management, and reporting
Quick-reference documentation delivered covering daily workflows and CRM hygiene

risks.name: Data Quality Risk
risks.impact: Contact data across multiple personal spreadsheets (500-1K contacts, 3 lists) may contain duplicates and inconsistent formatting, significantly increasing migration hours (W3 range: 10-20.5 hrs)
risks.mitigation: Send structured import template in W1; audit data quality before committing to migration plan; deduplicate in staging area; small test loads before full import

risks.name: Adoption Risk
risks.impact: 3-person PE advisory team accustomed to personal networks and memory-based relationship management may resist structured CRM usage, undermining ROI
risks.mitigation: Start with Outlook integration for immediate value (email/calendar sync); minimize required fields; automate capture; show PE health tracking value early; make CRM the easiest path, not extra work

risks.name: Data Submission Dependency
risks.impact: Team must submit raw data files by end of Week 2 or Week 3 migration timeline slips, compressing QA and pipeline work
risks.mitigation: Send data template during kickoff; set clear W2 deadline; follow up mid-week; if delayed, pull forward pipeline work from W4

risks.name: Scope Creep from GTM Assessment
risks.impact: GTM assessment identified 4 phases of work over 180+ days -- CRM is only phase 1. Client may want to pull demand gen, collateral, or positioning work into this engagement
risks.mitigation: Clearly scope this engagement as CRM infrastructure only (assessment phase 1); document future phases as separate engagements; use change order process for additions

assumptions: Office 365 / Outlook as primary email and calendar platform
500-1,000 contacts to migrate from personal spreadsheets and lists
PE firm parent-child company structure with labeled associations
Client provides raw data files by end of Week 2
1 data import test load before full migration
exclusions: GTM engine build (pitch deck, collateral, demand generation -- assessment phases 2-4)
Marketing automation, email campaigns, sequences, or landing pages
CFO/F&A launch positioning or service line branding
Ongoing managed services beyond 1-week post-launch async support
HubSpot Marketing Hub, Service Hub, or Operations Hub features
Integration with any system beyond Outlook (no ERP, no project management tools)
clientResponsibilities: Provide complete contact/company/deal data exports by end of Week 2
Designate Austin as primary point of contact for decisions and approvals
Ensure Austin, Sam, and Blake are available for kickoff, training, and UAT sessions
Procure HubSpot licenses before implementation begins
Provide Office 365 admin access for Outlook integration setup
Define buy-side and sell-side pipeline stages during Week 1
Review and sign off on imported data during Week 4
Complete UAT testing within agreed Week 6 timeline
Champion CRM adoption internally -- leadership sets expectations for daily usage
